title: cryptogramma crispa subsp. crispa
intro: Cryptogramma crispa subsp. crispa, also known as the golden rockfoil, is a species of fern in the family Cryptogrammaceae. It is a perennial plant that grows in the mountainous regions of western North America, characterized by its delicate fronds and golden-brown scales on the underside of its leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may help to treat wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The golden rockfoil can be prepared for medicinal use through infusions, tinctures, or salves, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its healing effects.
